ABA Merchant Web App.code:
def generate(payload, passcode, custom_data=None, key_order=None):
    import hmac
    import hashlib

    # payload = merged dict of request body JSON + Custom Data fields (if any)
    # custom_data = dict {key_name: value} - OPTIONAL, can be None or empty
    # key_order = list of key names to sign - OPTIONAL, defaults to all payload keys
    # passcode = HMAC secret key (can be empty string if target uses no key)
    
    debug_log = "--- HASHING PROCESS DEBUG ---\n"

    # 1. Determine Keys to Sign
    if key_order:
        keys_to_sign = key_order
        debug_log += "[1] Using Custom Keys Order: " + str(keys_to_sign) + "\n"
    else:
        keys_to_sign = [k for k in payload.keys() if k != 'hash']
        debug_log += "[1] Using Extracted Keys Order: " + str(keys_to_sign) + "\n"

    # 2. Concat Values
    concat_str = ""
    debug_log += "\n[2] Concatenating Values:\n"
    for k in keys_to_sign:
        val = payload.get(k)
        if val is None: val = ""
        debug_log += "    - {} = '{}'\n".format(k, val)
        concat_str += str(val)
        
    debug_log += "\n[3] Final Concatenated String:\n    -> '{}'\n".format(concat_str)

    # 3. Sign  (passcode can be empty string '' if target uses HmacSHA256(e, ''))
    debug_log += "\n[4] Generating HMAC-SHA256 signature...\n"
    debug_log += "    PassCode/Key length: {}\n".format(len(passcode))
    debug_log += "    Key used: '{}'\n".format(passcode)
    
    signature = hmac.new(
        passcode.encode('utf-8'),
        concat_str.encode('utf-8'),
        hashlib.sha256
    ).hexdigest()
    
    debug_log += "\n[5] Signature Result:\n    -> {}\n".format(signature)

    # Return tuple: (hash_result, debug_string)
    return signature, debug_log
